x-revision-notes: v1.1.0 (2026-04-22 Wave 10F): added `benchmark_trace` visual key (distinct muted grey-blue for reference / buy-and-hold comparison series — visually distinguishable from `equity_curve` which is reserved for the active strategy's own equity). Added `aliases` block providing semantic role names (`indicator`/`target`/`benchmark`) that resolve to the visual keys — consumed by VIZ-IC1 palette-conformance check. Per-asset-class benchmark split (`benchmark_equity`/`benchmark_fixed_income`/`benchmark_commodity`) is deferred until the portal actually hosts non-equity pairs (Ray's Wave-10F cross-review nuance, deferred to a future wave).
notes: Bootstrapped from the HY-IG v2 reference pair (per META-RPD) after the Wave 5 audit exposed palette inconsistency within the pair itself — the hero chart ships Okabe-Ito #D55E00 (vermillion) while zoom charts and some bar charts ship matplotlib defaults (#d62728 red, #1f77b4 blue, #2ca02c green). VIZ-V11 introduces this registry as the canonical palette source: every chart's `_meta.json` sidecar carries `palette_id`, and a pre-save lint blocks charts whose trace / shape / annotation colors resolve to raw matplotlib-plotly defaults instead of a registered palette role. Palette changes are methodological divergence per META-XVC.

okabe_ito_2026.description: Canonical colorblind-safe palette for AIG-RLIC+ portal charts (2026 vintage). Primary data in Okabe-Ito vermillion; secondary in Okabe-Ito blue; NBER shading at alpha 0.22 per VIZ-V2 rev 2026-04-19. Drop matplotlib #d62728 and similar defaults in favor of these roles.
okabe_ito_2026.source_citation: Okabe M, Ito K (2008), Color Universal Design — A Color Palette for All, J*FLY / Color Universal Design Organization.

aliases.x-note: Semantic role aliases → visual keys. Added 2026-04-22 (Wave 10F) so VIZ-IC1 palette-conformance check can assert role assignments without forcing producer scripts to know the underlying hex. Aliases are additive — visual keys remain the source of truth and can be referenced directly.
